title: Saudi Arabia–United Arab Emirates border - Wikipedia
content:
### Coordinates Information
The exact coordinates in degrees for the Ghuwaifat-Batha border crossing are not explicitly stated in the provided text.

### Geographical and Coastal Location Details

| Feature | Details and Data Points |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Western Border Terminus** | The border begins in the west on the coast at the **Sumayrah Gulf**. |
| **Coastal Peninsula** | The starting point is located just west of the UAE's **Ras Khumays (Ghumais)** peninsula. |
| **Border Segment 1** | Proceeds southwards from the coast for **26 km (16 mi)**. |
| **Coastal Corridor** | The Jeddah Agreement granted Saudi Arabia a **25 km (16 mi)** corridor eastwards from **Khor Al Adaid**. |
| **Gulf Access** | The corridor provides Saudi Arabia an outlet to the **Persian Gulf** on the eastern side of **Qatar**. |
| **Territorial Shift** | In 1977, Saudi Arabia gained **20 miles** east of **Ras Ghumais** on the Gulf coast. |
| **Physical Geography** | The border occasionally cuts across salt-flats such as the **Sabkhat Matti**. |

### Border Infrastructure and Site Details
*   **Border Crossing Name:** Ghuwaifat-Batha.
*   **Road Connection:** A road connecting Saudi territory with the UAE runs through **Al Sila**.
*   **Historical Road Closure:** In June 1990, the direct land road connecting the UAE and Qatar was closed; a new road was opened connecting Saudi territory to the UAE via Al Sila, replacing the old road connecting Abu Dhabi to the Qatari border.
*   **Proposed Infrastructure:** In 1976, Saudi Arabia hired a survey team to examine building a port at **Ras Ghumais**.
*   **Military Infrastructure:** Saudi Arabia built military infrastructure near the **Khor Al Udaid** inlet.
*   **Pipeline Projects:** The **Dolphin Gas Project** involves a pipeline through claimed territorial waters to supply gas from Qatar to the UAE and Oman.
*   **Causeway Project:** A proposed project intended to link **Doha** to **Abu Dhabi** was protested by Saudi Arabia for passing through its waters.
*   **Identification Requirements:** In August 2009, UAE citizens were turned back at the border because their identification cards displayed a map showing **Khor al-Udaid** and the **Zararah oilfield** as UAE territory.
